notes: Arturia Vox Continental V emulates the Vox Continental, the transistor combo organ synonymous with the British Invasion, used by The Animals, The Doors, Ray Manzarek, and The Dave Clark Five. Like the Farfisa, the Vox Continental uses transistor divider circuits rather than tonewheels, producing a bright, reedy, slightly nasal tone quite different from the Hammond's warm additive character. The distinctive inverted black-and-white keyboard, the independent upper and lower register tabs, and the built-in vibrato are its defining features. The Continental's sound is thinner and more cutting than the Farfisa, making it ideal for driving leads and punchy chordal stabs in 60s-inspired music, psychedelia, and garage rock.

Volume.description: Master volume — overall organ output level

Expression.description: Expression pedal — primary dynamic control for swell and volume variation

Modulation.description: Modulation wheel — vibrato depth or additional modulation

Upper Registers.description: Upper keyboard register tabs — combines footage tabs to shape timbre; the Continental's tabs are calibrated differently from the Farfisa, giving it a characteristically thinner, brighter top-end

Lower Registers.description: Lower keyboard register tabs — bass section footage tabs

Vibrato Rate.description: Vibrato rate — speed of the built-in vibrato; slow rates give gentle sway, faster rates give a churning Leslie-like effect

Vibrato Depth.description: Vibrato depth — intensity of pitch modulation; the Continental's vibrato is distinctive and recognisable

Tone.description: Tone/brightness — tonal shaping across the full organ output

Reverb.description: Reverb send level — spring or room reverb adds period character

Key Click.description: Key click level — transistor key click transient; adds realistic attack
